name: THOMAS WESTON ADAMS III
title:
Emergency Management Professional | Educator
Expert Communicator | Public Servant | Leader
statement: I work at the intersection of emergency management, communication, and community service. As a Section Supervisor at Kentucky Emergency Management, I steward the drawdown of the FEMA-funded Chemical Stockpile Emergency Preparedness Program (CSEPP) and serve in the State Emergency Operations Center during emergencies and disasters. I also serve with Wolfe County Search and Rescue as an officer, Wilderness First Responder, and certified rope and swiftwater rescue technician. Over 17 years of higher education teaching have prepared this generation's communication leaders—equipping them with speaking, critical thinking, and technology skills in classrooms across multiple universities and colleges. Nonprofit leadership in communication, outreach, and development grounds my work in audience-centered messaging, organizational stewardship, and public service. Some careers are built in one place. Mine has been built in the space between places—the classroom, the operations center, the disaster field, the community meeting room. I volunteer my time and energy where they matter most, staying committed to my personal goals and the relationships that fuel this work.
emergencyHighlights: Run the State EOC in five distinct roles — Planning Section Chief, Field Ops, Logistics, Public Safety Branch, and ESF5
Steward the drawdown of a multi-million-dollar FEMA program under full federal audit compliance (2 CFR 200, FAR 31.2)
Support the writing, updating, and implementation of all-hazards emergency plans and site-specific response plans
Coordinate multi-agency exercises and responses with law enforcement, fire, EMS, public health, military, and local and federal partners
Build and execute emergency preparedness exercises from tabletop to full-scale — then measure what worked
Deployed to Eastern Kentucky floods (2022) as a field responder under federal disaster declaration
Certified rope rescue, swift water rescue, and wilderness search technician — trained to operate where helicopters can't
Support public information efforts during emergencies and blue sky days for KYEM and SAR operations
Brief senior leadership and partner agencies with situation reports and after-action reviews that drive real change
Selected for Naval Postgraduate School executive education (2025) — one of the few in emergency management nationally
Provide perspective for my agency and its partners through an understanding of relational, organizational, social, and public communication
emergencyContent:
Kentucky Emergency Management (May 2023 – Present): Section Supervisor overseeing Chemical Stockpile Emergency Preparedness Program (CSEPP) stewardship and drawdown. Serve on State EOC team during activations in multiple roles: Planning Section Chief, Field Operations, Logistics, Public Safety Branch, and ESF5 Emergency Management Support Function. FEMA-funded position requiring high-level coordination with federal, state, and local partners including U.S. Army and National Guard.

Wolfe County Search & Rescue (January 2021 – Present): Officer, Treasurer, and Finance Officer for 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Active team member with certifications as Wilderness First Responder, Rope Rescue Technician, and Swift Water/Moving Water Rescue Technician. FEMA National Incident Management System (ICS) certified. Responded to Eastern Kentucky floods (2022) and numerous search operations.
educationHighlights: 17 years in the classroom across 10+ universities — from Pepperdine to Penn State to Shanghai University
Critical Thinking Teacher of the Year nominee (2015) and Teacher Who Made a Difference Award winner (2014)
Wrote a chapter on memes and pop culture communication for a book that won the National Communication Association Book Award (2023)
Tapped by Pearson Higher Ed as a Faculty Advisor — built and integrated learning technologies across multiple institutions
Trauma-informed, audience-centered, and relationship-focused communicator — from the classroom to the crisis scene
Qualified for nationals twice in competitive speech and debate — then spent a career teaching others to do the same
educationContent:
17+ Years of University-Level Teaching: Taught at 10+ institutions including Ohio Northern University (Visiting Assistant Professor), Texas A&M University (Full-time Faculty), Bluegrass Community & Technical College (Communications Area Faculty), University of Kentucky, Penn State, San Diego State, and internationally at Shanghai University. Designed curricula, mentored faculty, and coached hundreds of students through challenging communication scenarios.

Published Author & Award Winner: Author of "Memes as a Communicative Act" in Meme Life (2021), which won the National Communication Association Book Award (2023). Research Partner on Towers of Rhetoric: Memory and Reinvention (2018). Keynote speaker and presenter at national conferences.
publicServiceHighlights: Keep the books clean — manage financial reporting, reconciliation, and audit prep for a 501(c)(3) search and rescue team
Track every federal dollar from award to closeout — grant oversight, expenditure documentation, and funder reporting
Turn raw financial data into reports that boards, funders, and auditors actually understand
Ran for Kentucky State Representative (2022) — carried a campaign on public service, not politics
Built working relationships across law enforcement, fire, public health, military, and volunteer organizations that hold up under pressure
publicServiceContent:
Federal Grants Management Certificate: Completed comprehensive training in Subawarding for Pass-through Entities, Cost Principles (2 CFR 200 and FAR 31.2), Writing Successful Federal Grants, and Uniform Administrative Requirements for Federal Grants.

Nonprofit Leadership Experience: Currently serve as Treasurer & Finance Officer for Wolfe County Search & Rescue (501c3). Previously Executive Director and Interim President of Friends of Hemp (2019–2020), and Communication Director for Hemp Feed Coalition (2019–2020). Candidate for State Representative, Kentucky House District 73 (2022).
certificationsContent:
FEMA/Emergency Management Training: ICS 100, 200, 300, 305, 400, 700, 800; IS 2200 Basic EOC Functions; G 2300 Intermediate Emergency Operations; IS 29 Public Information Officer; IS 120 Introduction to Exercises; G-191 EOC Interface; IS 235 Emergency Planning; IS 240 Leadership and Influence; IS 241 Decision Making and Problem Solving; IS 242 Effective Communication; IS 244 Developing and Managing Volunteers; Chemical Incident Response (CHEMKPF AWR-947-W); WebPuff; IPAWS Alert System; WEB EOC Communication System

Search & Rescue Certifications: NOLS Wilderness First Responder; Swift Water Rescue; Technical Rope Rescue; B-Search and Rescue; Land Navigation, GPS & GAIA; Lost Person Behavior; Search Management

Executive Education: Radiological Emergency Preparedness Executive Education Program, Center for Homeland Defense and Security, Naval Postgraduate School (2025)
